steps.title: Read the period values from the table
steps.content:
The passage defines “period” as the time between consecutive pulses. The table lists five RRATs with these periods: J0614-03 = 0.136 s, J0103+54 = 0.354 s, J1654-2335 = 0.545 s, J0545-03 = 1.074 s, J0121+53 = 2.725 s. Now test each choice against these numbers.
  • Choice A — ‘J0614-03 has the shortest amount of time between consecutive pulses of all the RRATs in the table.’: 0.136 s is the smallest value in the table, so J0614-03 has the shortest period. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘J0545-03 and J0121+53 have the same amount of time between consecutive pulses.’: Their periods are 1.074 s and 2.725 s — not equal. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘J1654-2335 has the longest amount of time between consecutive pulses of all the RRATs in the table.’: J1654-2335’s period is 0.545 s; both J0545-03 and J0121+53 are longer. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘J0103+54 and J0121+53 both have more than one second of time between consecutive pulses.’: J0103+54’s period is only 0.354 s, well under one second. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: 0.136 seconds is the minimum period in the table, so J0614-03 unambiguously has the shortest time between consecutive pulses. That confirms A is correct.
